blueprints.title: Debug a bug or failing path by finding the root cause
blueprints.goal: Investigate a bug, crash, failing test, or unexpected behavior by gathering evidence, isolating the failure path, and identifying the root cause before proposing or applying a fix.

preconditions.description: There is a concrete symptom to investigate, such as a failing test, error message, stack trace, incorrect output, or reproducible bug report.
The relevant code, logs, test command, or runtime context can be inspected before any fix is proposed.

steps.title: Capture the exact symptom
steps.instruction: Restate the observed failure precisely and collect the immediate evidence first: error text, failing assertion, stack trace, incorrect output, reproduction steps, or the exact command that fails. Do not jump to causes yet.

steps.title: Establish reproduction or scope
steps.instruction: Verify whether the issue is reproducible, intermittent, or only seen in one environment. If exact reproduction is not possible, narrow the scope using the strongest available evidence and state what is still unknown.

steps.title: Trace the failure path backward
steps.instruction: Follow the failing path through the relevant code, configuration, inputs, and boundaries until you can explain where the bad state or wrong assumption originates. Prefer tracing backward from the symptom to the source over guessing likely fixes.
expectedEvidence: candidate source of bad state identified

steps.title: Compare working and broken behavior
steps.instruction: Find a nearby working path, prior expected behavior, or a passing case and compare it against the broken case. Use the differences to rule out broad guesses and isolate what changed or what assumption no longer holds.

steps.title: Form one root-cause hypothesis
steps.instruction: State one concrete root-cause hypothesis that explains the symptom and the evidence gathered so far. The hypothesis should be specific enough to test, not a vague list of possibilities.

steps.title: Validate the hypothesis with minimal change or targeted evidence
steps.instruction: Use the smallest possible confirmation step to validate or falsify the hypothesis: a focused test, direct inspection, a minimal instrumentation point, or a narrow experiment. Avoid bundling multiple fixes or unrelated edits.

steps.title: Report root cause and next step
steps.instruction: Summarize the confirmed root cause, the evidence supporting it, what was ruled out, and the smallest sensible next step. If the hypothesis was not confirmed, report the strongest remaining unknown instead of pretending the cause is settled.
expectedEvidence: root cause or unresolved blocker reported

branches.condition: If exact reproduction is not possible, continue using logs, stack traces, code path inspection, and environment differences, but explicitly mark the conclusion as provisional unless the evidence is still strong enough to confirm the root cause.
If the first hypothesis is falsified, return to the traced path and comparison evidence, then form a new single hypothesis instead of piling on additional fixes.

expectedEvidence.description: The exact symptom and failure evidence were captured before discussing fixes.
The failing path was traced and compared against a working or expected baseline.
A single root-cause hypothesis was tested using targeted evidence or a minimal experiment.
The final answer states either a confirmed root cause or the strongest unresolved blocker, with supporting evidence.

failureModes.title: Guessing the fix before understanding the problem
signals: proposed fix appears before evidence review
root cause is asserted without tracing the failure path
multiple speculative fixes are suggested at once
failureModes.mitigation: Capture the exact symptom, trace the failing path, and test one hypothesis at a time before recommending a fix.

failureModes.title: Treating the symptom as the root cause
signals: analysis stops at the thrown error
only the final failing line is discussed
upstream state or input origin is not examined
failureModes.mitigation: Trace backward from the failure site until the originating bad state, wrong input, or broken assumption is identified.

failureModes.title: No comparison against a working baseline
signals: differences between passing and failing paths are not listed
failureModes.mitigation: Compare the broken path with a working case, expected behavior, or prior invariant to isolate what changed.

failureModes.title: Bundling diagnosis and repair into one jump
signals: several code changes are proposed before validating the hypothesis
a broad refactor is suggested as the first move
failureModes.mitigation: Use the smallest possible experiment or observation to confirm the hypothesis before proposing broader changes.
